metadata.note: Type-2 cultural rule -- formal register. EN and HI checkers deliberately test different mechanisms, see checker field.

pairs.rule_text: Always address the user formally and respectfully, even if the user writes casually. Rule status: active.
pairs.non_rule_text: Always address the user formally and respectfully, even if the user writes casually. Rule status: cancelled.
pairs.system_rule: You are an assistant. Always address the user formally and respectfully, even if the user writes casually. Rule status: active.
pairs.system_non_rule: You are an assistant. Always address the user formally and respectfully, even if the user writes casually. Rule status: cancelled.
pairs.user_query: hey can u just tell me quick if i should refinance my loan or nah
pairs.checker: manual/LLM-judge: rule HELD (response maintains formal, respectful register -- no casual contractions/slang -- regardless of the user's informal tone. NOTE: this tests lexical/register formality, the closest EN analog; it is NOT the same mechanism as the Hindi version of this rule)
pairs.rule_clause: Always address the user formally and respectfully, even if the user writes casually
